title: Julie's Pasta Sauce Bases
ingredients: 2 cups whole milk or half and half
1/2 cup grated fresh parmesan, asiago or Romano cheese
instructions: Combine crushed tomatoes, olive oil, onion, and garlic in a large saucepan.
Add salt to taste and dash of cayenne pepper.
Top with freshly grated Parmesan, Romano or Asiago cheese.
If using onion, add onion and sauté for 5 minutes until soft.
Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds until aromatic.
Cook over medium heat while stirring for 1-2 minutes to make a roux.
Cook over medium heat, stirring or whisking frequently until thickened.
Add cheese and stir until well combined and melted.
Add salt and pepper to taste.
notes: Pasta is a fast meal that can incorporate almost any ingredients.
When using marinara sauce, heat milk in microwave on high for 2 minutes before adding.
Marinara sauce additions: meatballs, cooked Italian sausage, steamed zucchini or summer squash, chopped fresh spinach, sautéed mushrooms, or whatever else you desire.

Alfredo sauce additions: fresh spinach leaves chopped, sautéed mushrooms, lightly steamed vegetables (broccoli, squash, asparagus), cooked and diced chicken, cooked diced salmon, shrimp, crab or lobster, chopped fresh herbs, drained artichoke hearts, Cavendars Greek seasoning.
Alfredo sauce can be used alone or with additions of choice.
When the house was full of 10 people, the author made pasta at least once a week.
